data.title: Exxon prepares to wind down one of two steam crackers in Singapore - report
data.description:
ExxonMobil (XOM) plans to permanently shut down its older steam cracker on Jurong Island, Singapore, in March 2026, Reuters reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

The shutdown process is expected to be completed by June 2026, the sources added. The facility, which has been operational since 2002, faces closure amid global petrochemical overcapacity driven by Chinese production.

The move aligns with broader industry-wide losses, workforce reduction in Singapore, and sales of retail operations. 

Earlier this year, the company started a 1.6 million tons/year ethylene cracker in Huizhou, China, and also gradually reduced term contract volumes with customers in Singapore. The company will continue operating a second, newer cracker, started in 2013 on the island.

ExxonMobil (XOM) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: United Microelectronics' November revenue rose ~6% Y/Y
data.description:
• United Microelectronics (UMC) reported revenue of NT$21.23B for November 2025, marking a growth of 5.91% year-on-year.
  • For Jan.-Nov. 2025, revenue totaled NT$218.27B, up 2.31% Y/Y.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UP Fintech Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.31, touches record revenue of $175.2M
data.description:
• UP Fintech press release (TIGR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.31.
  • Revenue of $175.2M (+73.3% Y/Y)
  • GAAP EPADS of $0.27
  • Non-GAAP net income attributable to UP Fintech shareholders surged to US$57.0 million, up 28.2% QoQ and 2.8 times the level of the same period last year, also a record high.
  • Commissions were US$72.9 million, an increase of 76.9% from US$41.2 million in the same quarter of last year, due to an increase in trading volume.
  • Funded accounts increased by 31,500, bringing total funded clients up 18.5% YoY to 1.22 million.
  • Market activity remained robust, with Q3 trading volume climbing 28.5% YoY to US$209.4 billion.
  • Total client assets hit a new peak of US$61.0 billion, rising 17.3% sequentially and 49.7% YoY. 
  • "Tiger Vault" grew 339% YoY in AUC in Hong Kong

data.title: Salesforce outlines $41.45B–$41.55B revenue target for FY26 as Agentforce adoption accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Salesforce (CRM) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Marc Benioff, CEO, reported "we delivered strong results for the quarter across all of our key metrics" and highlighted the company’s ongoing execution toward its $60 billion long-term ambition. Benioff stated, "We have delivered incredible results with Agentforce. It's really exceeding our expectations... 3.2 trillion tokens delivered for our customers." He described Agentforce as Salesforce’s fastest-growing product ever, now reaching nearly $1.4 billion in ARR for the quarter, with Agentforce ARR at about $540 million, up 330% year-over-year. He also emphasized the successful integration of Informatica, Data 360, and MuleSoft, noting, "that’s going to strengthen our overall leadership in data and, of course, AI."

  • Benioff explained that the company is witnessing strong global demand for its agentic enterprise offerings, stating, "customers in production with Agentforce jumped 70% quarter-over-quarter," and described a “consumption flywheel” as more than 50% of new Agentforce and Data 360 bookings came from existing customers expanding investments.

  • Robin Washington, President and CFO, stated, "More than 70% of our top 100 wins included 5 or more clouds... new bookings for Agentforce 1 Edition and [A for X]... doubled quarter-over-quarter." Washington added, "Q3 was one of our biggest pipeline generation quarters ever and customers leveraging our forward-deployed engineers are seeing 33% faster deployment times."

Outlook

  • Washington stated, "We are reiterating our fiscal year '26 organic subscription and support growth guidance of approximately 9% year-over-year in constant currency."

  • The company expects total revenue for fiscal year ‘26 on an organic basis to be between $41.15 billion and $41.25 billion, with Informatica contributing to a consolidated total of $41.45 billion to $41.55 billion or approximately 9% to 10% growth in nominal terms and about 9% in constant currency.

  • Operating cash flow growth guidance was raised to "approximately 13% to 14% growth as a result of our strong Q3 bookings performance."

  • Organic current remaining performance obligation (cRPO) growth for Q4 is expected to be “approximately 11% year-over-year in nominal and 9% year-over-year in constant currency,” while inclusive of Informatica, cRPO growth is projected at 15% year-over-year in nominal, or 13% in constant currency.

Financial Results

  • Benioff reported Q3 revenue of $10.26 billion, up 9% year-over-year, and noted a non-GAAP operating margin of 35.5%.

  • Current remaining performance obligation (cRPO) was $29.4 billion, up 11% year-over-year. Operating cash flow for the quarter reached $2.3 billion, up 17% year-over-year, and free cash flow was $2.2 billion, up 22% year-over-year.

  • Washington highlighted that "Q3 non-GAAP operating margin [was] up 240 basis points and GAAP operating margin up 130 basis points."

  • Agentforce and Data 360 ARR increased 114% year-over-year, with Agentforce ARR up 330% year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Keith Weiss, Morgan Stanley: Asked about customer appetite for building generative AI solutions in-house versus using Salesforce. Miguel Milano, CRO, responded, "There is something very large, very important... every single company in the world, small, medium, large wants to become an agentic enterprise... they've been experimenting for 2 years. They've gone from experimentation now to frustration a little bit. And now they're all saying... this is hard... they all want to go to scale."

  • Raimo Lenschow, Barclays: Inquired about the expansion and ramping of sales reps. Milano stated, "We have now, today, 20% more capacity in place. We're going to finish the year with 15% more capacity enabled already... It takes 6 to 12 months to -- on average to ramp AEs."

  • Brad Zelnick, Deutsche Bank: Questioned Salesforce’s infrastructure business and competitive advantage. Benioff explained, "Our data infrastructure is incredible... Informatica, Data 360, our data cloud and also MuleSoft... together... will do about $10 billion next year in business."

  • Brent Thill, Jefferies: Asked about the "halo effect" of Agentforce on other clouds, especially Slack. Benioff responded, "It's an accelerator on the core... our ability to take our core applications, extend them and deliver another level of value beyond what we were doing before."

  • S. Kirk Materne, Evercore: Asked about Agentforce pricing and monetization as automation may impact customer headcount. Milano described flexible pricing models and noted, "We have the whole portfolio, and we are meeting... every customer is a different point of the journey."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts repeatedly probed for clarity on AI adoption, sales ramp, pricing, and monetization strategies, in a tone that was generally positive but focused on execution risks and the sustainability of growth, as reflected by questions on customer DIY efforts and headcount implications.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses. Benioff described the quarter as "the best quarter, I think we've had actually in 3 years," and Milano emphasized exponential monetization potential from agentic enterprise transformation.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased confidence, with less skepticism about AI adoption and more focus on operational execution and monetization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth decelerated slightly from 10% year-over-year in Q2 to 9% in Q3.

  • Agentforce ARR accelerated to $540 million, up 330% year-over-year, compared to broader AI and data ARR of $1.2 billion in Q2, up 120% year-over-year.

  • The integration of Informatica was completed three months ahead of schedule and is now included in guidance, whereas in Q2, Informatica contribution was excluded pending deal closure.

  • Sales capacity investments increased, with enabled capacity now up 23% for the year compared to a 20% increase at the end of Q2.

  • Management’s tone shifted from emphasizing early adoption and pilot-to-production transitions to highlighting broad-based adoption, operational scale, and exponential monetization opportunities.

  • Analysts’ questions shifted from structural risks and the impact of AI on SaaS models to operational execution, pricing models, and the sustainability of growth drivers.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited faster-than-anticipated mix shift to cloud for Tableau and on-prem revenue timing in Tableau and MuleSoft as partial offsets.

  • Washington noted geographic variability, with stronger growth in North America and EMEA but "Asia Pacific was more constrained, particularly in Australia and India."

  • Segment weakness was seen in "comms and media and manufacturing, automotive and energy."

  • Continued reallocation of resources to high-growth areas was mentioned as key to driving adoption and mitigating sector-specific softness.

Final Takeaway

The quarter demonstrated Salesforce's accelerating momentum in AI-driven solutions, with Agentforce and Data 360 driving record bookings, rapid enterprise adoption, and a strong pipeline for future growth. The successful integration of Informatica, flexible pricing models, and substantial increases in sales capacity position the company to capitalize on the secular shift toward agentic enterprise transformation. Management reiterated high confidence in sustainable, profitable growth, targeting up to $41.55 billion in revenue for fiscal year 2026 and substantial cash flow generation, supported by broad-based adoption and disciplined capital allocation strategies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Check Point Software prices private offering of $1.75B of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030
data.description:
• Check Point Software Technologies (NASDAQ:CHKP) announced  on Thursday the pricing of a $1.75B upsized private offering of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2030.
  • The offering was increased from the previously announced offering size of $1.5B.
  • The company has granted the initial purchasers of the notes an option to purchase up to an additional $250M amount of the notes for settlement within a 13-day period, including the date on which the notes are issued.
  • The sale of the notes to the initial purchasers is expected to settle on December 8, 2025.
  • The notes will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount will not accrete. The notes will mature on December 15, 2030.
  • The company intends to use $168M of the net proceeds to pay the costs of the capped call transactions, approximately $225M to repurchase, and approximately 1.18M ordinary shares pursuant to the company’s existing share repurchase program.
  • Furthermore, the firm intends to use the remainder of the net proceeds for general corporate purposes.
  • In connection with the pricing of the notes, Check Point expects to enter into privately negotiated capped call transactions with one or more of the initial purchasers of the offering.
  • Shares +2.06%.

data.title: Nvidia secures victory after Congress shoots down chip export curbs - report
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is on the verge of securing a massive lobbying win after U.S. lawmakers kept a measure out of must-pass defense legislation that would have limited the company’s ability to sell their advanced artificial intelligence chips to China and other adversary nations, Bloomberg News reported.

The proposed GAIN AI Act would require chipmakers such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to prioritize U.S. customers for advanced AI chips before selling to China or other arms-embargoed countries.

Lawmakers sought to advance the proposal as part of the annual defense policy bill, which is set to be released on Friday. A person familiar with the defense bill said it is not in the bill, though the situation could still change unexpectedly, the report said.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met with President Donald Trump and key lawmakers on Wednesday. Huang told reporters outside of Speaker Mike Johnson’s office that he was in town to “answer questions about AI.”

Later, Huang called lawmakers’ decision to not include GAIN AI in the defense bill “wise” and compared it to another policy Nvidia has fought against, the report said.

“The GAIN AI act is even more detrimental to the United States than the AI diffusion act,” Huang said.

Separately, Nvidia’s CEO criticized the idea of establishing a patchwork of state laws regulating AI.

data.title: Canfor to buy Canfor Pulp at $0.50 per share
data.description:
• Canfor Corporation (CFP:CA) to acquire all outstanding shares of Canfor Pulp Products (CFX:CA) not already owned by it or its affiliates, via a court-approved plan of arrangement.
  • Canfor (CFP:CA), which already owns about 54.8% of Canfor Pulp, will pay $0.50 per Canfor Pulp share—a 25% premium to the December 2 closing price and 38% to the 10-day volume-weighted average. 
  • The shareholder approval requires at least two-thirds of votes at a Canfor Pulp special meeting, with closing expected in Q1 2026.

data.title: Inotiv signals continued DSA revenue growth and margin improvements amid RMS site consolidations and robust awards momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Inotiv, Inc. (NOTV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Robert Leasure reported a strong year-over-year increase in demand for the Discovery & Safety Assessment (DSA) business, highlighting "a strong year-over-year increase in demand for our Discovery & Safety Assessment business" and sustained execution on goals from the May 2025 Investor Day, with a focus on "improving our cash flow and margins and maintaining our focus on customer metrics."
  • Leasure emphasized the ongoing RMS site consolidation, noting, "Phase 2 of our RMS site consolidation project has remained on track. In early October, we closed one of the three planned RMS facilities and now have two additional lease facilities remaining to close."
  • Addressing business disruptions, Leasure stated, "On August 18, we filed an 8-K disclosing that we became aware of a cybersecurity incident which caused disruption to certain of our business operations. We worked to restore availability and access to our networks and systems during this fiscal fourth quarter."
  • Leasure also announced engagement with Perella Weinberg Partners for debt refinancing alternatives and referenced settlements in pending litigation, explaining that settlement payments "will be fully covered by insurance."
  • CFO Beth Taylor provided key financial results: "For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, total revenue was $138.1 million compared to $130.4 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024. This was a $7.7 million or 5.9% increase in revenue from the prior year quarter, primarily driven by increased revenue of $7.1 million within our DSA segment."

Outlook

  • Management is not providing formal fiscal 2026 guidance at this time, with Taylor stating, "As we have stated previously, we hope to resume providing guidance once we have greater clarity on the market and client demand and clarity on any impact to our business once there is more information on tariffs."
  • Leasure noted ongoing positive trends into the first two months of the current quarter, saying, "we are coming off some very weak numbers from a year ago and the geopolitical and macroeconomic conditions, risk and uncertainties are likely to remain with the industry for the foreseeable future."

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q4 2025 reached $138.1 million, up $7.7 million or 5.9% year-over-year, with DSA revenue at $51.6 million, a 15.7% increase from the prior year period; RMS revenue was $86.5 million, up 0.8%.
  • DSA segment net new awards for Q4 were $54.2 million, a 61% year-over-year increase; the DSA backlog conversion rate reached 37.4% for the quarter—the highest in three years.
  • Operating loss for Q4 2025 was $6.8 million, narrowing from $13.2 million in Q4 2024; consolidated net loss attributable to common shareholders was $8.6 million or ($0.25) per diluted share, compared to ($0.73) per share last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 2025 was $11.8 million or 8.5% of revenue, more than doubling the prior year’s Q4 figure.
  • Cash from operations in Q4 was $14.3 million, raising the cash balance to $21.7 million by quarter end.

Q&A

  • Frank Takkinen, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC, Research Division: Asked about headwinds and the impact of cybersecurity expenses. Leasure responded, "the major headwind was for us in the early August finding out the cybersecurity incident we reported...it's really those intangible costs and the time it takes for organization to focus on that...it would be naive to think that it did have some impact on our earnings, our expenses and some of our awards."
  • Takkinen followed up on quarter-to-date trends. Leasure clarified, "our quarter ended December 30 is typically our weakest quarter during the holidays...we have an increase in awards of over 37% over the last 9 months."
  • Matthew Hewitt, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC, Research Division: Queried exposure to monoclonal antibodies in light of FDA guidance. Leasure stated, "our revenue related to monoclonal antibodies is minimal, very small if any."
  • Hewitt asked about customer budgets for the coming year. Leasure reported, "I do see an increasing amount of reoccurring customers, which is also very encouraging."
  • David Windley, Jefferies LLC, Research Division: Sought details on DSA lead times and RMS margin impact. Leasure explained, "large animal safety assessment capacity is operating at a very high level of capacity at the moment," and RMS margin fluctuations relate to "cost of the NHPs that we're bringing in" and associated operating expenses.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on headwinds, expense management, and sector uncertainties, with a neutral to slightly positive tone and some pressing for clarity on business disruptions and margin trends.
  • Management’s tone was generally confident and constructive, acknowledging challenges while emphasizing operational improvements and resilience. Leasure repeatedly expressed satisfaction with recent momentum, stating, "I was very pleased how quickly we recovered."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management displayed more confidence regarding DSA growth and margin improvements, while analyst sentiment remained cautiously optimistic.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company reported increased DSA revenue and awards momentum, with DSA revenue up 15.7% versus a prior 8.9% quarterly year-over-year gain, and awards up 61% from 25% in Q3.
  • RMS revenue growth slowed to 0.8% from 34.1% in Q3, but margins benefited from continued site consolidation and cost actions.
  • Operating loss narrowed significantly from the previous quarter, and adjusted EBITDA improved.
  • Management tone shifted from "cautiously optimistic" to more assertive confidence, citing improved metrics and ongoing operational gains.
  • Analysts continued to focus on sustainability of recent growth, execution risks, and sector headwinds, maintaining a similar level of skepticism as before.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing geopolitical and macroeconomic risks, as well as uncertainties around tariffs and sector demand.
  • The cybersecurity incident was highlighted as a significant headwind, with Leasure noting intangible and not fully quantifiable impacts on operations and expenses.
  • RMS margins remain subject to variability in NHP costs and external factors such as tariffs, transportation, and quarantine requirements.

Final Takeaway

The company highlighted strong DSA revenue and awards growth, sequential improvement in margins, and continued operational execution despite the temporary headwinds from a cybersecurity incident and challenging market conditions. Management remains focused on RMS site consolidations, technology streamlining, and debt refinancing, with a commitment to further improving margins and client experiences moving forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: MDU Resources prices public offering of 10.15M shares
data.description:
• MDU Resources (MDU) stated on Thursday that it has priced an underwritten public offering of 10.15M shares of its common stock at a price of $19.70 per share and an additional purchase option of 1.52M shares to its underwriters.
  • Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, and J.P. Morgan are acting as joint lead bookrunners of the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 5, 2025.
  • The shares are expected to be borrowed by the forward sellers, Wells Fargo Securities, BofA Securities, and J.P. Morgan from third parties and sold to the underwriters in the offering in connection with the forward sale agreements.
  • Settlement of the forward sale agreements is expected to occur no later than 24 months following the completion of the offering.
  • If the company elects physical settlement of the forward sale agreements, it expects to use any net proceeds for general corporate purposes like repayment of debt, capital expenditures, and acquisitions, including payment in 2026 for a 49% undivided ownership interest in the Badger Wind Farm project, as well as working capital.
